title: General method for obtaining the energy minimum of spin Hamiltonians for separable states
abstract: We present a general method to determine the energy minimum of spin Hamiltonians over separable states when the single-particle reduced density matrices are fixed. For ferromagnetic Ising and Ising-like models with nearest-neighbor interactions on lattices of any dimension and on a fully connected graph in an external field, this minimum is given by a compact analytic formula involving the quantum Fisher information. For the ferromagnetic Heisenberg chain of spin-1/2 particles, the minimum is expressed via the Uhlmann-Jozsa fidelity. These relations enable the direct extraction of both the quantum Fisher information and the fidelity from correlation measurements on the ground states of suitably engineered spin models.
summary: This paper presents a general method to calculate the minimum energy of spin Hamiltonians for separable states using local constraints. It establishes direct analytical links between ground-state energy correlations and key quantum information quantities like the Quantum Fisher Information and fidelity.
main_problem: Determining the energy minimum of spin Hamiltonians over separable states when single-particle reduced density matrices are fixed, and finding if information measures like QFI can be extracted from correlation measurements.
main_result: The authors derive compact analytic formulas for energy minima involving Quantum Fisher Information (QFI) and Uhlmann-Jozsa fidelity, and prove that energy bounds for separable states can be saturated by specific lattice configurations.
method: The study employs analytical derivations, semidefinite programming for numerical optimization, and mathematical frameworks such as the de Finetti theorem and the properties of convex/concave roofs of variances.
model_or_system: The paper considers N-particle spin systems (qudits) on various topologies, including 1D chains, 2D lattices (cubic, hexagonal), and fully connected graphs, specifically focusing on ferromagnetic Ising and Heisenberg models.
key_observables: Quantum Fisher Information (QFI), Uhlmann-Jozsa fidelity, Wigner-Yanase skew information, two-particle correlations, and single-particle reduced density matrices.
important_parameters: Interaction strength (J), external magnetic field (B), particle number (N), and k-producibility.
main_assumptions: The method focuses on separable, symmetric separable, and k-producible states; certain results assume two-colorable lattices or periodic boundary conditions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 visualizes spin models on different lattices and state types; Figure 2 compares ground state energies and QFI bounds; Figure 3 illustrates energy bounds for k-producible states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of energy minimization, presents theorems for energy bounds and saturation on specific lattices, provides analytical links to QFI and fidelity, and discusses applications to k-producible states and entanglement detection.
why_interesting: It provides a direct way to extract fundamental quantum information metrics like QFI and fidelity from measurable correlation data in engineered spin models, which is highly relevant for quantum metrology and many-body characterization.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. (a) Spin models on two-colorable graphs. (top) Spin chain. (left) Two-dimensional cubic lattice. (right) Hexago- nal lattice. (b) N-particle separable state with two-particle nearest-neighbor marginals ϱAB and ϱBA. (top) Two-particle separable state ϱAB with marginals ϱ. (bottom) A portion of an N-particle chain in which all single-particle reduced states are ϱ and all nearest-neighbor two-particle reduced states are ϱAB or ϱBA defined in Eq. (8). (c) N-particle symmetric separable state with two-particle nearest-neighbor marginals ϱAB described in Theorem 3. (top) Two-particle symmet- ric separable state ϱAB with single-particle reduced states ϱ. (bottom) Symmetric separable...

Figure 2. (a) Ground state energy and bounds for the ferro- magnetic Ising spin chain (N = 10, periodic boundary con- dition) with J = 1 and ⃗B = (Bx, 0, 0), and plotted as a function of ⟨Jx⟩. (solid) Energy minimum, i.e., ground state energy. (dashed) Lower bound for separable states based on the quantum Fisher information. It is also an upper bound on the energy minimum. (dotted) Lower bound on the ground state energy based on the Wigner-Yanase skew infor- mation. Inset: The same quantities for the expectation value of the correlation terms, without the single-particle terms. (b) Quantum Fisher information from correlation measure- ments. (solid) FQ[ϱ, σz/2] as a function of |⟨σx⟩ϱ|,...

Figure 3. Energy bounds for k-producible states for an Ising spin chain with J = 1 for (a) ⟨σx⟩ϱ = 0.1 and (b) 0.3. (dia- mond) Bound for a tensor product of k-particle states given in Eq. (22). (circle) Lower bound for k-producible states with the quantum Fisher information of the single particle state Eq. (21). (square) Lower bound for the energy per particle with the Wigner-Yanase skew information. (dotted) Energy minimum per qubit for infinite systems [80].
